Untitled Project
GHOST Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Requirements
Deep Traceability
  • VIEWS
  • Document
  • Table
  • Traceability
  • Deep Traceability

    • 1. 1. Purpose and Scope
    • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
      2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
    1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-PRODUCT-NAME
      1.2. Product and Package Naming Boundary GHOST-MEM-IR-PRODUCT-NAME The product described by this document shall be named GHOST; current Python implementation paths may remain under ragstream.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-MEMORY-PARENT
      1.3. Memory Management Parent GHOST-MEM-IR-MEMORY-PARENT Memory Ingestion and Retrieval shall be governed by the authority model, capability boundaries, configuration governance, and sibling ownership defined in 50_memory_management.sdoc.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-RECORDING-DEPENDENCY
      1.4. Durable Recording Dependency GHOST-MEM-IR-RECORDING-DEPENDENCY Memory ingestion shall consume MemoryRecords owned by 51_memory_recording_and_files.sdoc and shall not replace their durable truth.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-MERGE-HANDOFF
      1.5. Compression and Synthesis Handoff GHOST-MEM-IR-MERGE-HANDOFF Runtime episodic compression, ActiveBrief behavior, and final Memory Context synthesis shall be refined by 53_memory_merge_activebrief_blackboard.sdoc.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-BOUNDARY
      1.6. Ingestion and Retrieval Boundary GHOST-MEM-IR-BOUNDARY This document shall own memory vector-entry construction, memory vector persistence, ingestion orchestration, semantic vector search, deterministic index lookup, scoring, candidate selection, MemoryContextPack construction, and SuperPrompt memory-artifact write-back.

      It shall not own document retrieval, document A3/A4, durable MemoryRecord body changes, or final user-answer generation.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-CHUNKER
      2.1. MemoryChunker Path GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-CHUNKER Memory vector-entry construction shall be traceable to ragstream/memory/ingestion/memory_chunker.py.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-INGEST-MANAGER
      2.2. MemoryIngestionManager Path GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-INGEST-MANAGER Memory ingestion orchestration shall be traceable to ragstream/memory/ingestion/memory_ingestion_manager.py.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-STORE
      2.3. MemoryVectorStore Path GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-STORE Dedicated memory-vector persistence shall be traceable to ragstream/memory/ingestion/memory_vector_store.py.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-RETRIEVER
      2.4. MemoryRetriever Path GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-RETRIEVER Memory retrieval orchestration shall be traceable to ragstream/retrieval/retriever_mem.py.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-LOOKUP
      2.5. MemoryIndexLookup Path GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-LOOKUP Deterministic SQLite/.ragmem lookup shall be traceable to ragstream/memory/retrieval/memory_index_lookup.py.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-SCORER
      2.6. MemoryScorer Path GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-SCORER Semantic hit normalization, parent aggregation, tag policy, recency, and semantic-chunk selection shall be traceable to ragstream/memory/retrieval/memory_scoring.py.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-PACK
      2.7. MemoryContextPack Path GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-PACK The runtime memory candidate container shall be traceable to ragstream/memory/retrieval/memory_context_pack.py.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-CONTROLLER
      2.8. Controller Wiring Path GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-CONTROLLER MemoryRetriever configuration and invocation shall be traceable to ragstream/app/controller.py and ragstream/app/ui_streamlit.py.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-DIR
      2.9. Current Memory Vector Directory GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-DIR Current startup shall persist memory vectors under data/memory/vector_db/.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-COLLECTION
      2.10. Current Memory Collection GHOST-MEM-IR-COLLECTION Current startup shall use the Chroma collection name memory_vectors for memory vector entries.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-EMBEDDER
      2.11. Current Memory Vector Embedding Model GHOST-MEM-IR-EMBEDDER Current Streamlit startup shall initialize the memory vector store with OpenAI text-embedding-3-large.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-SEPARATE-STORE
      2.12. Document Store Separation GHOST-MEM-IR-SEPARATE-STORE The memory vector store shall remain physically and logically separate from project document Chroma stores and SPLADE stores.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-ROLES
      3.1. Vector Entry Roles GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-ROLES MemoryChunker shall produce vector entries with role record_handle, question, or answer.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ONE-HANDLE
        3.2. One Record Handle GHOST-MEM-IR-ONE-HANDLE Each record ingestion shall attempt to create one record_handle entry with block_id 0000 and position zero.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-PROJECT
          3.8. Record Handle Project Text GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-PROJECT The current record-handle embedded text shall include PROJECT derived from active_project_name.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-YAKE
          3.9. Record Handle Automatic Keywords GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-YAKE The current record-handle embedded text shall include YAKE_KEYWORDS derived from automatic keywords.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-QUESTION
          3.10. Record Handle Question Anchor GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-QUESTION The current record-handle embedded text shall include a QUESTION_ANCHOR derived from the first question block and bounded by question_anchor_tokens.
          • GHOST-MEM-IR-QUESTION-ANCHOR-TRUNCATE
            4.8. Question Anchor Truncation GHOST-MEM-IR-QUESTION-ANCHOR-TRUNCATE Question-anchor truncation shall retain the first configured number of whitespace-delimited words.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-NO-TAG-TEXT
          3.11. Current Handle Text Exclusions GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-NO-TAG-TEXT The current record-handle embedded text shall not include tag or user_keywords, although those values may remain present in vector metadata.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-QUESTION-BLOCKS
        3.3. Question Entries GHOST-MEM-IR-QUESTION-BLOCKS MemoryChunker shall create ordered question entries from input_text, beginning with block_id 0001 and position one.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ANSWER-BLOCKS
        3.4. Answer Entries GHOST-MEM-IR-ANSWER-BLOCKS MemoryChunker shall create ordered answer entries from output_text, beginning with block_id 0001 and position one.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-EMPTY-ENTRY-FILTER
        3.5. Empty Entry Filter GHOST-MEM-IR-EMPTY-ENTRY-FILTER A generated vector entry whose document text is empty after trimming shall be omitted from the returned entry list.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ID-FORMAT
        3.6. Vector Entry Identifier GHOST-MEM-IR-ID-FORMAT The current vector identifier shall follow mem::<file_id>::<record_id>::<role>::<block_id>.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-ID-TRACEABILITY
          3.7. Vector Parent Traceability GHOST-MEM-IR-ID-TRACEABILITY Every vector entry identifier and metadata shall link to file_id and record_id.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-METADATA
        3.12. Vector Entry Metadata Contract GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-METADATA Each vector entry shall carry current file and record identifiers, paired filenames, parent_id, role, block identity and position, character offsets, approximate token count, tag, project, source, creation time, hashes, keyword text, embedded-file snapshot text, chunking configuration version, and ingestion hash.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-METADATA-SNAPSHOT
          3.13. Vector Metadata Snapshot GHOST-MEM-IR-METADATA-SNAPSHOT Vector metadata shall represent values at ingestion time and shall remain derivative rather than current metadata authority.
          • GHOST-MEM-IR-LIVE-METADATA-OVERLAY
            3.14. Live Metadata during Retrieval GHOST-MEM-IR-LIVE-METADATA-OVERLAY When live MemoryRecords are available, retrieval scoring and candidate enrichment shall overlay live record metadata onto vector-derived metadata.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-ENTRIES
        17.3. AC: Vector Entry Roles GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-ENTRIES Given one non-empty MemoryRecord, ingestion shall create one non-empty record handle and the expected ordered non-empty question and answer entries with stable traceable IDs.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-CHUNKER-CONFIG
      4.1. Current Chunker Configuration GHOST-MEM-IR-CHUNKER-CONFIG MemoryChunker shall maintain current defaults config_version memory_chunker_001, target_tokens 500, max_tokens 800, and question_anchor_tokens 220, with optional constructor overrides.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-TOKEN-APPROX
        4.2. Approximate Token Counting GHOST-MEM-IR-TOKEN-APPROX Current MemoryChunker token counts shall be the number of non-whitespace word matches and shall not be described as exact provider-token counts.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-PARAGRAPH-UNITS
        4.3. Paragraph Unit Selection GHOST-MEM-IR-PARAGRAPH-UNITS Paragraphs not exceeding max_tokens shall be used as semantic units.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-SENTENCE-UNITS
        4.4. Oversized Paragraph Sentence Units GHOST-MEM-IR-SENTENCE-UNITS An oversized paragraph shall be split into current sentence-like units based on punctuation or line endings.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-HARD-SPLIT
        4.5. Oversized Unit Hard Split GHOST-MEM-IR-HARD-SPLIT A semantic unit exceeding max_tokens shall be hard-split by non-whitespace word groups of at most max_tokens approximate tokens.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-BLOCK-TARGET
        4.6. Target Block Completion GHOST-MEM-IR-BLOCK-TARGET Current units shall accumulate until target_tokens is reached or the next unit would exceed max_tokens.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-BLOCK-OFFSETS
        4.7. Block Offsets GHOST-MEM-IR-BLOCK-OFFSETS Every question or answer block shall retain start_offset and end_offset relative to its source side.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-CHUNK-CONFIG-JSON-DIRECTION
        4.11. Chunker JSON Migration Direction GHOST-MEM-IR-CHUNK-CONFIG-JSON-DIRECTION Memory chunk sizes, anchor limit, and chunker version are operational tuning values and shall migrate to validated versioned JSON configuration before being claimed as centrally configured.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-INGESTION-HASH
      4.9. Ingestion Hash GHOST-MEM-IR-INGESTION-HASH MemoryChunker shall compute SHA-256 over a deterministic JSON payload describing file_id, record_id, input/output hashes, tag, automatic/user keywords, active project, and chunking configuration version.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-INGESTION-HASH-NOT-TRUTH
        4.10. Ingestion Hash Status GHOST-MEM-IR-INGESTION-HASH-NOT-TRUTH The ingestion hash shall identify the derivative ingestion representation and shall not replace input_hash, output_hash, record_id, or file_id.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-PERSISTENT
      5.1. Persistent Chroma Store GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-PERSISTENT MemoryVectorStore shall create its persistence directory and open or create one named Chroma collection.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-NO-TRUTH
        5.2. Vector Store Non-Authority GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-NO-TRUTH MemoryVectorStore shall not own MemoryRecord truth, SQLite metadata authority, memory scoring, or MemoryContextPack construction.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-REPLACE-ID
        5.3. Record Replacement Identifier Validation GHOST-MEM-IR-REPLACE-ID replace_record_entries shall reject an empty record_id.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-REPLACE-DELETE-FIRST
        5.4. Current Record Replacement Order GHOST-MEM-IR-REPLACE-DELETE-FIRST Current record replacement shall count and delete all existing vectors for record_id before embedding and adding the new entry set.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-REPLACEMENT-ATOMICITY
          5.16. Current Replacement Transaction Boundary GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-REPLACEMENT-ATOMICITY Current delete-then-add replacement shall not be described as one atomic storage transaction; durable MemoryRecord truth shall provide failure isolation when vector replacement is interrupted.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-REPLACE-EMPTY
        5.5. Empty Replacement Entry Set GHOST-MEM-IR-REPLACE-EMPTY When replacement receives no entries, it shall leave the record with zero vectors and return a successful zero-write report after deleting prior vectors.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-DOCUMENTS
        5.6. Embedded Documents GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-DOCUMENTS The memory vector collection shall persist the vector entry document text together with embeddings and sanitized metadata.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-METADATA-SANITIZE
        5.7. Metadata Sanitization GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-METADATA-SANITIZE Vector metadata shall be converted to Chroma-compatible scalar values; non-scalar values shall be JSON-serialized and None shall become an empty string.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-EMBED
        5.8. Document Embedding GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-EMBED MemoryVectorStore shall obtain one embedding per vector-entry document through its configured embedder and normalize returned vectors to lists of floats.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-ADD
        5.9. Vector Add Contract GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-ADD After prior record vectors are deleted, current replacement shall add aligned IDs, documents, embeddings, and metadata to the collection.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-REPLACE-REPORT
        5.10. Replacement Report GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-REPLACE-REPORT Record replacement shall report success, record_id, deleted prior vector count, vectors written, resulting record vector count, collection name, and persistence directory.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-QUERY-EMPTY
        5.11. Empty Store Query GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-QUERY-EMPTY MemoryVectorStore.query shall return an empty hit list when query_text is empty after trimming.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-QUERY
        5.12. Store Query Contract GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-QUERY MemoryVectorStore.query shall embed query_text, query the collection with optional sanitized flat metadata filter, and return ordered flat hits containing id, document, metadata, distance, and one-based rank.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-DELETE-RECORD
        5.13. Delete Record Vectors GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-DELETE-RECORD MemoryVectorStore.delete_record shall delete vectors by record_id and report deleted and remaining counts.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-DELETE-FILE
        5.14. Delete History Vectors GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-DELETE-FILE MemoryVectorStore.delete_file shall delete vectors by file_id and report deleted and remaining file-level counts.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-COUNT-SAFE
        5.15. Vector Count Failure Behavior GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-COUNT-SAFE Current count_record and count_file helpers shall return zero when collection counting raises an exception.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-DEPS
      6.1. Ingestion Dependencies GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-DEPS MemoryIngestionManager shall receive the active MemoryManager, MemoryChunker, and MemoryVectorStore.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-ID
      6.2. Ingest Record Identifier Validation GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-ID ingest_record shall return an unsuccessful result when record_id is empty.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-LOOKUP
      6.3. Live Record Lookup GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-LOOKUP ingest_record shall resolve record_id from the active MemoryManager.records list and shall report unsuccessful status when not found.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-BUILD
      6.4. Vector Entry Construction GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-BUILD For a found record, ingestion shall call MemoryChunker.build_vector_entries with current file_id and paired filenames.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-ROLE-COUNTS
      6.5. Ingestion Role Counts GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-ROLE-COUNTS Ingestion shall count returned entries by metadata role for diagnostics and result reporting.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-REPLACE
      6.6. Record Vector Replacement GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-REPLACE Ingestion shall replace all vectors for the record_id using the newly generated entry set.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-IDEMPOTENT-REPRESENTATION
        6.16. Record-Level Re-Ingestion Idempotency GHOST-MEM-IR-IDEMPOTENT-REPRESENTATION Re-ingesting a record shall replace its prior record_id vectors rather than intentionally append duplicate representations.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-IDEMPOTENT
          17.4. AC: Record Re-Ingestion GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-IDEMPOTENT Re-ingesting the same record_id shall replace prior vectors and shall not increase the number of duplicate IDs for that record.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-RESULT
      6.7. Ingestion Result Enrichment GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-RESULT The ingestion result shall include role counts, current file_id, and active .ragmem filename in addition to store replacement data.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-EXCEPTION
      6.8. Ingestion Exception Handling GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-EXCEPTION An exception during vector-entry construction or store replacement shall return an unsuccessful record-level result and shall not modify durable MemoryRecord files.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-ALL
      6.9. Loaded History Bulk Ingestion GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-ALL ingest_all shall iterate the current active MemoryManager record list in order, invoke ingest_record for each record, and report total, success, failure, and individual results.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-ALL-STATUS
        6.10. Bulk Ingestion Success Status GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-ALL-STATUS The bulk ingestion success field shall be true only when failure_count is zero.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-SCHEDULE
      6.11. Asynchronous Record Ingestion GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-SCHEDULE ingest_record_async shall schedule one daemon thread for a valid active record_id.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-DUPLICATE
        6.12. Duplicate Async Suppression GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-DUPLICATE A process-local lock and active-record identifier set shall prevent concurrent duplicate scheduling for the same record_id within one MemoryIngestionManager instance.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-CLEANUP
        6.13. Async Active-Set Cleanup GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-CLEANUP The asynchronous worker shall remove record_id from the active set in a finally block.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-STREAMLIT-CONTEXT
        6.14. Optional Streamlit Thread Context GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-STREAMLIT-CONTEXT When available, current scheduling shall attach the active Streamlit script context to the ingestion thread; absence of that support shall not block thread start.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-NO-DURABLE-QUEUE
        6.15. No Durable Ingestion Queue Claim GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-NO-DURABLE-QUEUE The current daemon-thread scheduler shall not be described as a durable queue, retry service, or cross-process ingestion coordinator.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVER-DEPS
      7.1. MemoryRetriever Dependencies GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVER-DEPS MemoryRetriever shall construct MemoryIndexLookup, MemoryScorer, MemoryCompressor, and MemoryMergeSynthesizer from its supplied dependencies and configuration.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-SHAPE
        7.2. Full or Section Configuration Input GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-SHAPE MemoryRetriever shall accept either the full runtime configuration containing memory_retrieval or the memory_retrieval section itself.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ENABLED
        7.3. Memory Retrieval Enable Switch GHOST-MEM-IR-ENABLED When memory_retrieval.enabled is false, MemoryRetriever.run shall skip its processing and return the SuperPrompt unchanged.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-MEMORY-PERMISSION
        7.4. PreProcessing Memory Permission GHOST-MEM-IR-MEMORY-PERMISSION Memory retrieval and Memory Context synthesis shall honor the deterministic allow_memory_context and memory_context_policy prepared by PreProcessing.

        A disabled memory-context policy shall suppress memory context creation rather than allow unrelated memory to enter the final prompt.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-PERMISSION
          17.15. AC: Memory Context Permission GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-PERMISSION When PreProcessing sets allow_memory_context false, the final SuperPrompt shall not receive synthesized memory context for that request.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-MISSING-FILE-ID
        7.5. Missing Active file_id GHOST-MEM-IR-MISSING-FILE-ID When active file_id is empty, MemoryRetriever shall write an empty MemoryContextPack with reason no_active_memory_file.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PRIMARY
        7.6. Primary Memory Query GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PRIMARY MemoryRetriever shall use non-empty SuperPrompt.effective_retrieval_query_text as its primary query source.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PROJECTOR
          7.7. Projected Query Fallback GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PROJECTOR When the effective query is empty, MemoryRetriever shall attempt SuperPromptProjector.build_query_text.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-BODY-FALLBACK
          7.8. Body Query Fallback GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-BODY-FALLBACK If projector query construction fails, current fallback shall concatenate non-empty task, purpose, context, and text body fields in that order.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PROMPT-FALLBACK
          7.9. Prompt-Ready Final Fallback GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PROMPT-FALLBACK If no fallback body fields are available, current query construction may use non-empty prompt_ready.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-EMPTY-QUERY
        7.10. Empty Query Result GHOST-MEM-IR-EMPTY-QUERY When no query text can be built, MemoryRetriever shall write an empty pack with reason empty_query_text.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-KEY-INPUT
        7.11. Direct Recall Key Input GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-KEY-INPUT Current MemoryRetriever shall inspect SuperPrompt extras and body for direct_recall_key or memory_direct_recall_key and shall use the first non-empty trimmed value.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-KEY-NO-UI
          7.12. Current Direct Recall Query UI Boundary GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-KEY-NO-UI The current GUI does not require a separate Direct Recall query field; an absent key shall produce no Direct Recall lookup.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-LATEST-BRIEF
        7.13. Latest Eligible ActiveBrief Snapshot GHOST-MEM-IR-LATEST-BRIEF MemoryRetriever shall scan active records newest-to-oldest and select the latest non-Black record with a non-empty ActiveRetrievalBrief as the runtime brief snapshot.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-SEMANTIC-ENABLE
      8.1. Semantic Memory Enable Switch GHOST-MEM-IR-SEMANTIC-ENABLE When semantic_memory_chunks.enabled is false, the semantic pass shall return empty raw hits, scored hits, parent scores, semantic chunks, and semantic episodic candidates.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-SEMANTIC-MAX
      8.2. Semantic Chunk Limit GHOST-MEM-IR-SEMANTIC-MAX The final semantic memory chunk list shall be capped by semantic_memory_chunks.max_memory_chunks whose current JSON baseline is 5.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-RAW-HIT-LIMIT
      8.3. Current Raw Hit Expansion GHOST-MEM-IR-RAW-HIT-LIMIT The current raw vector hit request shall use max(max_memory_chunks multiplied by 6, 20) to provide additional evidence for parent aggregation.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-RAW-HIT-CONFIG-DIRECTION
        8.4. Raw Hit Expansion Configuration Direction GHOST-MEM-IR-RAW-HIT-CONFIG-DIRECTION The raw-hit expansion multiplier and minimum are tuning parameters and shall migrate to validated versioned JSON before being claimed as centrally configured.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-SEMANTIC-FILE-FILTER
      8.5. Automatic Semantic History Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-SEMANTIC-FILE-FILTER Automatic semantic memory retrieval shall be restricted to the active file_id.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-FILTER-FAILURE
        8.6. Filtered Query Failure Integrity GHOST-MEM-IR-FILTER-FAILURE Failure of the active-file metadata filter shall not authorize automatic unfiltered retrieval across all histories.

        Cross-history semantic fallback shall be treated as non-conforming unless separately approved.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-SCOPE
          17.12. AC: Automatic History Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-SCOPE Automatic semantic and deterministic retrieval shall not return a record from another history solely because the active-file filter failed.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-EMBED
      8.7. Memory Query Embedding GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-EMBED The memory query shall be embedded once through the memory vector store embedder for the raw semantic query.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-RAW-HIT-SHAPE
      8.8. Raw Hit Shape GHOST-MEM-IR-RAW-HIT-SHAPE Normalized raw vector hits shall contain id, document, metadata, distance, and one-based rank.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-LIVE-EPISODE-METADATA
      8.9. Episode Distance Metadata GHOST-MEM-IR-LIVE-EPISODE-METADATA Live MemoryRecords shall be assigned episode_index and episode_distance_k where the latest record has k zero and each older record increments k by one.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-K-NOT-CLOCK
        8.10. Episode Distance Not Clock Time GHOST-MEM-IR-K-NOT-CLOCK Current recency shall be based on record distance k in the active history rather than elapsed wall-clock time.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-SEMANTIC-PARENT
      8.11. Semantic Parent Candidate Conversion GHOST-MEM-IR-SEMANTIC-PARENT Ranked parent scores shall be converted into episodic candidates with live Q/A body when available and SQLite/.ragmem fallback when not available in RAM.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-DISTANCE-SCORE
      9.1. Distance-to-Score Conversion GHOST-MEM-IR-DISTANCE-SCORE When a raw hit has no explicit score, current scoring shall convert Chroma distance d to 1 divided by 1 plus d after clamping negative d to zero.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-DISTANCE-INVALID
        9.2. Invalid Distance Handling GHOST-MEM-IR-DISTANCE-INVALID Missing, non-numeric, NaN, or infinite distance shall produce score zero.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-SCORED-HIT
      9.3. Scored Hit Contract GHOST-MEM-IR-SCORED-HIT Each scored hit shall preserve rank, vector_id, record_id, role, score, semantic_score, distance, document, metadata, and raw hit.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-GROUP-BY-RECORD
      9.4. Parent Grouping GHOST-MEM-IR-GROUP-BY-RECORD Parent aggregation shall group scored hits by non-empty record_id.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROLE-MAX
        9.5. Current Per-Role Aggregation GHOST-MEM-IR-ROLE-MAX For each parent MemoryRecord, current aggregation shall use the maximum question hit score, maximum answer hit score, and maximum other-role meta score.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-NO-PNORM-PARENT
          9.6. No Current Parent p-Norm Claim GHOST-MEM-IR-NO-PNORM-PARENT The current parent aggregation shall not be described as p-norm averaging across multiple hits within a role.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-PARENT-SCORE
          17.8. AC: Parent Role Scoring GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-PARENT-SCORE Given multiple hits for one parent and role, the current parent role score shall equal the maximum role hit and final semantic score shall apply the configured QA/Q/A weights.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-PARENT-DIAGNOSTICS
        9.7. Parent Hit Diagnostics GHOST-MEM-IR-PARENT-DIAGNOSTICS Each parent score record shall retain hit_count and the contributing hit list for diagnostics.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-SOURCE-MODE-QA
        9.8. QA Parent Weighting GHOST-MEM-IR-SOURCE-MODE-QA For retrieval_source_mode QA, current configured parent scoring shall apply answer, question, and meta weights from runtime_config; the current baseline is 0.55, 0.35, and 0.10.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-SOURCE-MODE-A
        9.9. A Parent Weighting GHOST-MEM-IR-SOURCE-MODE-A For retrieval_source_mode A, current baseline parent weights shall be answer 0.85, question 0.0, and meta 0.15.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-SOURCE-MODE-Q
        9.10. Q Parent Weighting GHOST-MEM-IR-SOURCE-MODE-Q For retrieval_source_mode Q, current baseline parent weights shall be answer 0.0, question 0.85, and meta 0.15.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-SOURCE-MODE-FALLBACK
        9.11. Invalid Source Mode Fallback GHOST-MEM-IR-SOURCE-MODE-FALLBACK An invalid parent retrieval_source_mode shall be normalized to QA for scoring.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-SEMANTIC-PARENT-SCORE
        9.12. Semantic Parent Score GHOST-MEM-IR-SEMANTIC-PARENT-SCORE semantic_parent_score shall be the weighted sum of answer_score, question_score, and meta_score under the applied source-mode weights.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-EXCLUDED-TAGS
      9.13. Automatic Retrieval Excluded Tags GHOST-MEM-IR-EXCLUDED-TAGS Parent and semantic-chunk automatic selection shall exclude tags collected from configured memory retrieval exclude_tags; current configuration excludes Black.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-GOLD-FLAG
      9.14. Gold Recency Bypass Flag GHOST-MEM-IR-GOLD-FLAG A non-excluded Gold parent shall be marked as bypassing normal recency scoring.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-EPISODIC-RECENCY-ENABLE
      10.1. Episodic Recency Enable Switch GHOST-MEM-IR-EPISODIC-RECENCY-ENABLE When episodic_memory.recency_enabled is false, final parent score shall equal semantic_parent_score for non-Gold candidates.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-EPISODIC-WEIGHTS
      10.2. Current Episodic Semantic and Recency Weights GHOST-MEM-IR-EPISODIC-WEIGHTS Current Green episodic scoring shall use configured semantic and recency weights whose baselines are 0.75 and 0.25.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-WEIGHT-NORMALIZATION
      10.3. Two-Weight Normalization GHOST-MEM-IR-WEIGHT-NORMALIZATION Semantic and recency weights shall be clamped non-negative and normalized to sum one; when their total is non-positive, the effective weights shall become 1.0 semantic and 0.0 recency.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-RECENCY-HALF-LIFE
      10.4. Episode-Distance Half-Life GHOST-MEM-IR-RECENCY-HALF-LIFE Current recency score shall use 2 raised to negative k divided by half_life_k; the current episodic baseline half-life is 10 records.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-RECENCY
        17.9. AC: Episode Recency GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-RECENCY For Green records at k zero and k equal to half-life, recency scores shall be 1.0 and 0.5 before semantic/recency weighted combination.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-RECENCY-MISSING
      10.5. Missing Episode Distance GHOST-MEM-IR-RECENCY-MISSING Missing, invalid, NaN, or infinite episode distance shall produce recency score 1.0 so otherwise relevant memory is not discarded solely by absent metadata.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-RECENCY-NEGATIVE
      10.6. Negative Episode Distance GHOST-MEM-IR-RECENCY-NEGATIVE Negative episode distance shall be treated as zero.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-RECENCY-HALF-LIFE-FALLBACK
      10.7. Invalid Half-Life Fallback GHOST-MEM-IR-RECENCY-HALF-LIFE-FALLBACK Invalid, non-positive, NaN, or infinite half-life shall use the current fallback 10.0.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-GOLD-RECENCY
      10.8. Gold Parent Recency Bypass GHOST-MEM-IR-GOLD-RECENCY Gold parent final score shall equal semantic_parent_score and shall carry no recency score in the current scoring path.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-PARENT-RANK
      10.9. Parent Deterministic Ranking GHOST-MEM-IR-PARENT-RANK Parent candidates shall be sorted by final_parent_score descending.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-SEMANTIC-ROLES
      10.10. Semantic Chunk Evidence Roles GHOST-MEM-IR-SEMANTIC-ROLES Final semantic memory chunks shall be selected only from question and answer hits; record_handle hits shall remain discovery evidence and shall not be returned as semantic evidence chunks.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-SEMANTIC
        17.11. AC: Semantic Chunk Roles GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-SEMANTIC Final semantic memory chunks shall contain question/answer evidence only and shall exclude record_handle entries.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-SEMANTIC-WEIGHTS
      10.11. Current Semantic Chunk Weights GHOST-MEM-IR-SEMANTIC-WEIGHTS Current non-Gold semantic chunk scoring shall use configured semantic and recency weights whose baselines are 0.90 and 0.10.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-SEMANTIC-HALF-LIFE
      10.12. Semantic Chunk Half-Life GHOST-MEM-IR-SEMANTIC-HALF-LIFE Current semantic chunk recency shall use configured half_life_k whose baseline is 10 records.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-SEMANTIC-GOLD
      10.13. Gold Semantic Chunk Recency Bypass GHOST-MEM-IR-SEMANTIC-GOLD A Gold semantic chunk shall use semantic_score directly without recency contribution.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-SEMANTIC-SORT
      10.14. Semantic Chunk Ranking GHOST-MEM-IR-SEMANTIC-SORT Semantic chunk candidates shall be sorted by final score descending and truncated to max_memory_chunks.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-SEMANTIC-TRACE
      10.15. Semantic Chunk Trace Fields GHOST-MEM-IR-SEMANTIC-TRACE Selected semantic chunks shall preserve vector_id, record_id, role, final and semantic scores, recency data, distance, document text, merged metadata, and raw rank.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-LOOKUP-NO-SQLITE
      11.1. Missing SQLite Behavior GHOST-MEM-IR-LOOKUP-NO-SQLITE When memory_index.sqlite3 does not exist, deterministic lookup shall return no rows and log the missing-index condition.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-WORKING-ENABLE
      11.2. Working Memory Enable Switch GHOST-MEM-IR-WORKING-ENABLE When working_memory.enabled is false, working-memory lookup shall return an empty list.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-WORKING-CURRENT-FILE
      11.3. Working Memory History Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-WORKING-CURRENT-FILE Working-memory lookup shall be restricted to the active file_id.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-WORKING-LATEST
      11.4. Working Memory Ordering and Limit GHOST-MEM-IR-WORKING-LATEST Working-memory lookup shall select latest non-excluded records ordered by created_at_utc descending and limited by max_pairs; the current baseline is 2.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-WORKING
        17.6. AC: Working Memory GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-WORKING Given an active history with more records than max_pairs, working lookup shall return only the latest configured non-excluded records from that history.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-GOLD-ENABLE
      11.5. Gold Lookup Enable Dependency GHOST-MEM-IR-GOLD-ENABLE When episodic_memory.enabled is false, current Gold lookup shall return an empty list.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-GOLD-CURRENT-FILE
      11.6. Gold History Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-GOLD-CURRENT-FILE Gold lookup shall be restricted to the active file_id.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-GOLD-LATEST
      11.7. Gold Ordering and Limit GHOST-MEM-IR-GOLD-LATEST Gold lookup shall select latest Gold records ordered by created_at_utc descending and limited by max_gold_records; the current baseline is 1.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-ENABLE
      11.8. Direct Recall Enable Switch GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-ENABLE When direct_recall.enabled is false or the key is empty, Direct Recall lookup shall return no candidate.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-EXACT
      11.9. Exact Direct Recall Match GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-EXACT Direct Recall shall match direct_recall_key by exact SQLite equality rather than semantic similarity.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-CROSS-FILE
        11.10. Direct Recall Cross-History Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-CROSS-FILE Direct Recall may search all indexed memory histories and is the current explicit cross-history retrieval path.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-EXCLUDE
        11.11. Direct Recall Tag Exclusion GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-EXCLUDE Direct Recall shall exclude configured tags; the current baseline excludes Black.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-PRIORITY
        11.12. Direct Recall Result Priority GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-PRIORITY When multiple exact matches exist, current ordering shall prefer Gold, then Green, then other tags, and then latest created_at_utc.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-DIRECT
          17.7. AC: Exact Direct Recall GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-DIRECT Given the same Direct Recall Key in multiple histories, lookup shall return at most one non-excluded result using tag priority and recency ordering.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-ONE
        11.13. One Direct Recall Candidate GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-ONE Current Direct Recall lookup shall return at most one candidate.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-LOOKUP-FILE-ROW
      11.14. File Metadata Attachment GHOST-MEM-IR-LOOKUP-FILE-ROW Deterministic candidates shall attach the corresponding memory_files row and paired history title and filenames.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-LOOKUP-BODY
      11.15. .ragmem Body Attachment GHOST-MEM-IR-LOOKUP-BODY Because SQLite stores metadata only, deterministic candidates shall obtain input_text and output_text by scanning the paired .ragmem file for matching record_id.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-LOOKUP-MISSING-BODY
        11.16. Missing Body Behavior GHOST-MEM-IR-LOOKUP-MISSING-BODY When the paired filename, record_id, file, or matching block is unavailable, current body attachment shall return empty body fields rather than fabricate Q/A.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-LOOKUP-JSON-LIST
      11.17. Indexed JSON List Parsing GHOST-MEM-IR-LOOKUP-JSON-LIST Keyword and file-snapshot SQLite JSON fields shall parse to lists when valid and shall become empty lists when invalid.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-EPISODIC-ENABLE
      12.1. Episodic Memory Enable Switch GHOST-MEM-IR-EPISODIC-ENABLE When episodic_memory.enabled is false, semantic parent scores shall not be converted into episodic candidates.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-EPISODIC-CAP
      12.2. Episodic Candidate Cap GHOST-MEM-IR-EPISODIC-CAP Semantic parent conversion and final episodic merge shall be bounded by episodic_memory.max_total_records; the current baseline is 3.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-EPISODIC-LIVE-BODY
      12.3. Live Record Candidate Enrichment GHOST-MEM-IR-EPISODIC-LIVE-BODY When a selected parent record is present in MemoryManager.records, candidate content shall be enriched from the live MemoryRecord full view.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-EPISODIC-FILE-BODY
      12.4. Indexed Record Candidate Fallback GHOST-MEM-IR-EPISODIC-FILE-BODY When a selected parent is absent from live RAM, MemoryRetriever shall attempt SQLite/.ragmem reconstruction for that record_id within the active file.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-EPISODIC-MERGE-ORDER
      12.5. Gold-First Episodic Merge GHOST-MEM-IR-EPISODIC-MERGE-ORDER Final episodic merging shall process deterministic Gold candidates before semantic episodic candidates.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-EPISODIC-DEDUPE
      12.6. Episodic Record Deduplication GHOST-MEM-IR-EPISODIC-DEDUPE Final episodic merging shall remove duplicate record_id values while preserving the Gold-first source order.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-GOLD
        17.10. AC: Gold Priority and Deduplication GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-GOLD A Gold record also selected semantically shall appear once in final episodic candidates and shall retain Gold-first source priority.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-EPISODIC-SOURCE
      12.7. Episodic Source Annotation GHOST-MEM-IR-EPISODIC-SOURCE Merged episodic candidates shall carry episodic_source equal to gold or semantic according to the selected path.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-WORKING-ENRICH
      12.8. Working Candidate Live Enrichment GHOST-MEM-IR-WORKING-ENRICH Working-memory candidates shall be overlaid with current live MemoryRecord content and episode-distance metadata when matching live records exist.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-GOLD-ENRICH
      12.9. Gold Candidate Live Enrichment GHOST-MEM-IR-GOLD-ENRICH Gold candidates shall be overlaid with current live MemoryRecord content and episode-distance metadata when matching live records exist.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-ENRICH
      12.10. Direct Recall Live Enrichment GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-ENRICH A Direct Recall candidate shall be overlaid with current live MemoryRecord content when the selected record belongs to the active in-memory record set.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK
      13.1. MemoryContextPack Runtime Container GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK MemoryRetriever shall construct one MemoryContextPack for each non-skipped or explicit-empty memory retrieval result.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-WORKING
        13.2. Working Candidate List GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-WORKING MemoryContextPack shall maintain working_memory_candidates as an ordered list.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-EPISODIC
        13.3. Episodic Candidate List GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-EPISODIC MemoryContextPack shall maintain episodic_candidates as an ordered list.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-SEMANTIC
        13.4. Semantic Chunk List GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-SEMANTIC MemoryContextPack shall maintain semantic_memory_chunks as an ordered list.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-DIRECT
        13.5. Direct Recall Candidate GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-DIRECT MemoryContextPack shall maintain zero or one direct_recall_candidate.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-CONTEXT
        13.6. Synthesized Memory Context GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-CONTEXT MemoryContextPack shall maintain synthesized_memory_context and memory_synthesis_diagnostics.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-SELECTION-DIAG
        13.7. Selection Diagnostics GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-SELECTION-DIAG MemoryContextPack shall maintain selection_diagnostics explaining query, counts, and scoring policy or explicit empty reason.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-TOKEN-REPORT
        13.8. Token Budget Report GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-TOKEN-REPORT MemoryContextPack shall maintain a token_budget_report whose current raw-memory estimate is max(1, len(serialized pack) divided by 4).
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-TOKEN-APPROX
          13.9. Approximate Token Report GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-TOKEN-APPROX The current len(json)//4 estimate shall be identified as rough diagnostics rather than exact provider tokenization.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-COUNTS
        13.10. Candidate Counts GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-COUNTS MemoryContextPack.counts shall report list lengths and binary presence of Direct Recall and synthesized context.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-PACK
          17.13. AC: MemoryContextPack GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-PACK After one successful memory retrieval, the SuperPrompt shall contain a MemoryContextPack whose candidate counts match its actual lists and optional Direct Recall/context fields.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-DICT
        13.11. Pack Dictionary Projection GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-DICT MemoryContextPack.to_dict shall project all candidate groups, synthesis fields, diagnostics, token report, and counts.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-DEBUG
        13.12. Raw Debug Markdown GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-DEBUG MemoryContextPack may render raw candidates and diagnostics as developer debug Markdown, explicitly labeled as not final prompt context.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-NO-DURABLE
        13.13. Pack Non-Persistence GHOST-MEM-IR-PACK-NO-DURABLE MemoryContextPack shall remain run-local and shall not be written into durable MemoryRecord files or SQLite truth.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-CONTROLLER-CONFIG
      14.1. MemoryRetriever Configuration GHOST-MEM-IR-CONTROLLER-CONFIG AppController.configure_memory_retrieval shall construct MemoryRetriever after MemoryManager and MemoryVectorStore exist and shall pass the controller memory SQLite path and runtime configuration.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-CONTROLLER-ORDER
      14.2. Current Retrieval Call Order GHOST-MEM-IR-CONTROLLER-ORDER AppController.run_retrieval shall run document Retriever first and then call MemoryRetriever when configured.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-CONTROLLER-FAILURE
      14.3. Controller Memory Retrieval Failure Isolation GHOST-MEM-IR-CONTROLLER-FAILURE An exception from MemoryRetriever during AppController.run_retrieval shall be logged and shall not erase the already produced document retrieval result.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-CONTROLLER-NOT-CONFIGURED
      14.4. Unconfigured MemoryRetriever GHOST-MEM-IR-CONTROLLER-NOT-CONFIGURED When MemoryRetriever is not configured, the controller shall skip memory retrieval and return the SuperPrompt after document retrieval.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-SP-PACK
      14.5. SuperPrompt Memory Pack GHOST-MEM-IR-SP-PACK MemoryRetriever shall assign the MemoryContextPack object to SuperPrompt.memory_context_pack.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-SP-CONTEXT
      14.6. SuperPrompt Memory Context GHOST-MEM-IR-SP-CONTEXT MemoryRetriever shall assign synthesized_memory_context to SuperPrompt.memory_context_text.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-SP-BRIEF
      14.7. SuperPrompt Active Memory Brief GHOST-MEM-IR-SP-BRIEF MemoryRetriever shall assign the latest eligible ActiveBrief title and body to SuperPrompt.active_memory_brief_title and active_memory_brief.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-SP-EXTRAS
      14.8. SuperPrompt Memory Extras GHOST-MEM-IR-SP-EXTRAS MemoryRetriever shall store pack dictionary, memory context text, debug Markdown, counts, and ActiveBrief title/body in SuperPrompt.extras under current memory keys.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-SP-NO-DOCUMENT-OVERWRITE
      14.9. Document Artifact Preservation GHOST-MEM-IR-SP-NO-DOCUMENT-OVERWRITE Memory write-back shall not overwrite base_context_chunks, document retrieval/reranking/A3/A4 views, final_selection_ids, or S_CTX_MD.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-SEPARATE
        17.14. AC: Document Artifact Separation GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-SEPARATE Running memory retrieval shall leave document chunk objects, stage views, and S_CTX_MD unchanged.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-SP-NO-STAGE
      14.10. Current Stage Ownership GHOST-MEM-IR-SP-NO-STAGE Current MemoryRetriever write-back shall not independently replace the SuperPrompt document-stage lifecycle label or document stage history.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-EMPTY-PACK-WRITE
      14.11. Empty Pack Write-Back GHOST-MEM-IR-EMPTY-PACK-WRITE For missing file_id or empty query, MemoryRetriever shall write an empty pack and latest eligible ActiveBrief snapshot into SuperPrompt.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-NORMAL-PREVIEW
      14.12. Normal Preview Uses Synthesized Context GHOST-MEM-IR-NORMAL-PREVIEW The normal SuperPrompt projector shall render memory_context_text and shall not render raw memory candidate debug Markdown by default.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-ROOT
      15.1. Memory Retrieval Configuration GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-ROOT Memory retrieval settings shall be loaded from the versioned runtime_config.json memory_retrieval section.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-PARENT-WEIGHTS
        15.2. Consumed Parent Score Weights GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-PARENT-WEIGHTS Current MemoryScorer shall consume parent_score_weights for QA, Q, and A modes.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-WORKING
        15.3. Consumed Working Settings GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-WORKING Current working-memory lookup shall consume enabled, max_pairs, and exclude_tags.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-EPISODIC
        15.4. Consumed Episodic Settings GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-EPISODIC Current episodic processing shall consume enabled, max_total_records, max_gold_records, Green semantic/recency weights, Green half-life, recency_enabled, and exclude_tags.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-DIRECT
        15.5. Consumed Direct Recall Settings GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-DIRECT Current Direct Recall shall consume enabled and exclude_tags; its current interface inherently returns at most one key match.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-SEMANTIC
        15.6. Consumed Semantic Chunk Settings GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-SEMANTIC Current semantic processing shall consume enabled, max_memory_chunks, semantic/recency weights, half-life, recency_enabled, and exclude_tags.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-TOKEN-LIMITS-NOT-ENFORCED
        15.7. Current Retrieval Token Fields Not Enforced Here GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-TOKEN-LIMITS-NOT-ENFORCED The current retrieval path shall not claim enforcement of working-memory, Gold, Green, or Direct Recall max-token fields merely because they exist in runtime_config.json.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-GOLD-BOOLEAN
        15.8. Gold Bypass Configuration Truth GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-GOLD-BOOLEAN Current Gold recency bypass is implemented by scoring logic; the separate gold_bypass_recency JSON fields shall not be claimed as dynamically controlling that behavior unless the implementation reads them.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-TAG-CATALOG
        15.9. Tag Catalog Configuration Truth GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-TAG-CATALOG MemoryManager currently defines its tag catalog in Python; runtime_config.memory_retrieval.tag_catalog shall not be claimed as the active catalog source until it is consumed.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-MIGRATION
        15.10. Operational Parameter Migration GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-MIGRATION Chunking values, raw-hit expansion, collection/model startup values, and remaining retrieval limits shall migrate to validated versioned JSON before being reported as centrally configured.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-CONFIG
        17.16. AC: Configuration Consumption Truth GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-CONFIG Changing an unconsumed JSON field shall not be represented as changing runtime behavior; changing a consumed field shall be observable in selection or diagnostics.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-NO-FABRICATED-HIT
      16.1. No Fabricated Vector Hit GHOST-MEM-IR-NO-FABRICATED-HIT Missing vector collection, embedder, SQLite row, .ragmem body, or live record shall produce missing/empty candidate data rather than invented content.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVAL-FAIL-TRUTH
      16.2. Retrieval Failure and Durable Truth GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVAL-FAIL-TRUTH A memory retrieval failure shall not modify .ragmem, .ragmeta.json, SQLite record truth, or stored memory vectors.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-FAIL-TRUTH
      16.3. Ingestion Failure and Durable Truth GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-FAIL-TRUTH A memory ingestion failure shall leave the durable MemoryRecord body and current metadata intact.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-ASYNC-FAIL
        17.5. AC: Ingestion Failure Isolation GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-ASYNC-FAIL If vector embedding or replacement fails, the corresponding .ragmem block and metadata shall remain available as durable truth.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-NO-CROSS-HISTORY-AUTO
      16.4. No Unapproved Automatic Cross-History Retrieval GHOST-MEM-IR-NO-CROSS-HISTORY-AUTO Automatic working, Gold, semantic parent, and semantic chunk retrieval shall remain active-history scoped; only exact Direct Recall may cross histories in the current design.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-NO-SECRET
      16.5. No Secret Vector Metadata GHOST-MEM-IR-NO-SECRET Memory vector documents and metadata shall not intentionally include provider API keys or browser credentials.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-PUBLIC-LOG
      16.6. Public Retrieval Logging GHOST-MEM-IR-PUBLIC-LOG Public logs shall report compact counts and statuses and shall not expose complete memory bodies, query embeddings, or vector values by default.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIDENTIAL-LOG
      16.7. Confidential Retrieval Diagnostics GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIDENTIAL-LOG Complete raw hits, candidates, scoring snapshots, and pack payloads shall remain behind explicitly enabled confidential developer logging.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-SCORE-REPRODUCIBILITY
      16.8. Scoring Reproducibility GHOST-MEM-IR-SCORE-REPRODUCIBILITY Diagnostics shall preserve effective scoring policy, candidate counts, query text, and active file identity sufficiently to reproduce selection decisions without exposing credentials.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-DETERMINISTIC-TESTS
      16.9. Deterministic Retrieval Testability GHOST-MEM-IR-DETERMINISTIC-TESTS Vector ID generation, chunk splitting, metadata sanitation, distance conversion, role aggregation, recency decay, tag filtering, deterministic lookups, candidate merge, and pack projection shall be independently testable.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-NO-DOC-A3
      16.10. No Memory-to-Document A3 Claim GHOST-MEM-IR-NO-DOC-A3 Memory semantic chunks shall not be marked with document A3ChunkStatus or inserted into document A3/A4 stage views in the current implementation.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-CODE-OWNERSHIP
      17.1. Ingestion and Retrieval Code Ownership GHOST-MEM-IR-CODE-OWNERSHIP MemoryChunker, MemoryIngestionManager, MemoryVectorStore, MemoryRetriever, MemoryIndexLookup, MemoryScorer, and MemoryContextPack shall map to the deepest applicable UIDs in this document.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-SYNC
        17.17. AC: Requirement-Code Synchronization GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-SYNC A memory ingestion/retrieval baseline shall not be accepted when vector identity, history scope, scoring equations, tag policy, candidate roles, pack semantics, or configuration authority contradicts this document.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-SIBLING-BOUNDARY
      17.2. Sibling Ownership Boundary GHOST-MEM-IR-SIBLING-BOUNDARY Durable recording/files shall remain owned by 51; ActiveBrief algorithms, runtime compression, and synthesis shall remain owned by 53.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-PRODUCT-NAME
    1.2. Product and Package Naming Boundary GHOST-MEM-IR-PRODUCT-NAME The product described by this document shall be named GHOST; current Python implementation paths may remain under ragstream.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-MEMORY-PARENT
    1.3. Memory Management Parent GHOST-MEM-IR-MEMORY-PARENT Memory Ingestion and Retrieval shall be governed by the authority model, capability boundaries, configuration governance, and sibling ownership defined in 50_memory_management.sdoc.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-RECORDING-DEPENDENCY
    1.4. Durable Recording Dependency GHOST-MEM-IR-RECORDING-DEPENDENCY Memory ingestion shall consume MemoryRecords owned by 51_memory_recording_and_files.sdoc and shall not replace their durable truth.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-MERGE-HANDOFF
    1.5. Compression and Synthesis Handoff GHOST-MEM-IR-MERGE-HANDOFF Runtime episodic compression, ActiveBrief behavior, and final Memory Context synthesis shall be refined by 53_memory_merge_activebrief_blackboard.sdoc.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-BOUNDARY
    1.6. Ingestion and Retrieval Boundary GHOST-MEM-IR-BOUNDARY This document shall own memory vector-entry construction, memory vector persistence, ingestion orchestration, semantic vector search, deterministic index lookup, scoring, candidate selection, MemoryContextPack construction, and SuperPrompt memory-artifact write-back.

    It shall not own document retrieval, document A3/A4, durable MemoryRecord body changes, or final user-answer generation.

    2. 2. Implementation Location Map and Startup Wiring
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-CHUNKER
    2.1. MemoryChunker Path GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-CHUNKER Memory vector-entry construction shall be traceable to ragstream/memory/ingestion/memory_chunker.py.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
