    GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-INGEST-MANAGER
    2.2. MemoryIngestionManager Path GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-INGEST-MANAGER Memory ingestion orchestration shall be traceable to ragstream/memory/ingestion/memory_ingestion_manager.py.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-STORE
    2.3. MemoryVectorStore Path GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-STORE Dedicated memory-vector persistence shall be traceable to ragstream/memory/ingestion/memory_vector_store.py.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-RETRIEVER
    2.4. MemoryRetriever Path GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-RETRIEVER Memory retrieval orchestration shall be traceable to ragstream/retrieval/retriever_mem.py.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-LOOKUP
    2.5. MemoryIndexLookup Path GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-LOOKUP Deterministic SQLite/.ragmem lookup shall be traceable to ragstream/memory/retrieval/memory_index_lookup.py.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-SCORER
    2.6. MemoryScorer Path GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-SCORER Semantic hit normalization, parent aggregation, tag policy, recency, and semantic-chunk selection shall be traceable to ragstream/memory/retrieval/memory_scoring.py.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-PACK
    2.7. MemoryContextPack Path GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-PACK The runtime memory candidate container shall be traceable to ragstream/memory/retrieval/memory_context_pack.py.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-CONTROLLER
    2.8. Controller Wiring Path GHOST-MEM-IR-PATH-CONTROLLER MemoryRetriever configuration and invocation shall be traceable to ragstream/app/controller.py and ragstream/app/ui_streamlit.py.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-DIR
    2.9. Current Memory Vector Directory GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-DIR Current startup shall persist memory vectors under data/memory/vector_db/.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-COLLECTION
    2.10. Current Memory Collection GHOST-MEM-IR-COLLECTION Current startup shall use the Chroma collection name memory_vectors for memory vector entries.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-EMBEDDER
    2.11. Current Memory Vector Embedding Model GHOST-MEM-IR-EMBEDDER Current Streamlit startup shall initialize the memory vector store with OpenAI text-embedding-3-large.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-SEPARATE-STORE
    2.12. Document Store Separation GHOST-MEM-IR-SEPARATE-STORE The memory vector store shall remain physically and logically separate from project document Chroma stores and SPLADE stores.

    3. 3. Memory Vector Entry Model
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-ROLES
    3.1. Vector Entry Roles GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-ROLES MemoryChunker shall produce vector entries with role record_handle, question, or answer.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ONE-HANDLE
      3.2. One Record Handle GHOST-MEM-IR-ONE-HANDLE Each record ingestion shall attempt to create one record_handle entry with block_id 0000 and position zero.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-PROJECT
        3.8. Record Handle Project Text GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-PROJECT The current record-handle embedded text shall include PROJECT derived from active_project_name.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-YAKE
        3.9. Record Handle Automatic Keywords GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-YAKE The current record-handle embedded text shall include YAKE_KEYWORDS derived from automatic keywords.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-QUESTION
        3.10. Record Handle Question Anchor GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-QUESTION The current record-handle embedded text shall include a QUESTION_ANCHOR derived from the first question block and bounded by question_anchor_tokens.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-QUESTION-ANCHOR-TRUNCATE
          4.8. Question Anchor Truncation GHOST-MEM-IR-QUESTION-ANCHOR-TRUNCATE Question-anchor truncation shall retain the first configured number of whitespace-delimited words.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-NO-TAG-TEXT
        3.11. Current Handle Text Exclusions GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-NO-TAG-TEXT The current record-handle embedded text shall not include tag or user_keywords, although those values may remain present in vector metadata.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-QUESTION-BLOCKS
      3.3. Question Entries GHOST-MEM-IR-QUESTION-BLOCKS MemoryChunker shall create ordered question entries from input_text, beginning with block_id 0001 and position one.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ANSWER-BLOCKS
      3.4. Answer Entries GHOST-MEM-IR-ANSWER-BLOCKS MemoryChunker shall create ordered answer entries from output_text, beginning with block_id 0001 and position one.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-EMPTY-ENTRY-FILTER
      3.5. Empty Entry Filter GHOST-MEM-IR-EMPTY-ENTRY-FILTER A generated vector entry whose document text is empty after trimming shall be omitted from the returned entry list.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ID-FORMAT
      3.6. Vector Entry Identifier GHOST-MEM-IR-ID-FORMAT The current vector identifier shall follow mem::<file_id>::<record_id>::<role>::<block_id>.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ID-TRACEABILITY
        3.7. Vector Parent Traceability GHOST-MEM-IR-ID-TRACEABILITY Every vector entry identifier and metadata shall link to file_id and record_id.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-METADATA
      3.12. Vector Entry Metadata Contract GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-METADATA Each vector entry shall carry current file and record identifiers, paired filenames, parent_id, role, block identity and position, character offsets, approximate token count, tag, project, source, creation time, hashes, keyword text, embedded-file snapshot text, chunking configuration version, and ingestion hash.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-METADATA-SNAPSHOT
        3.13. Vector Metadata Snapshot GHOST-MEM-IR-METADATA-SNAPSHOT Vector metadata shall represent values at ingestion time and shall remain derivative rather than current metadata authority.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-LIVE-METADATA-OVERLAY
          3.14. Live Metadata during Retrieval GHOST-MEM-IR-LIVE-METADATA-OVERLAY When live MemoryRecords are available, retrieval scoring and candidate enrichment shall overlay live record metadata onto vector-derived metadata.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-ENTRIES
      17.3. AC: Vector Entry Roles GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-ENTRIES Given one non-empty MemoryRecord, ingestion shall create one non-empty record handle and the expected ordered non-empty question and answer entries with stable traceable IDs.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-ROLES
      3.1. Vector Entry Roles GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-ROLES MemoryChunker shall produce vector entries with role record_handle, question, or answer.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-ONE-HANDLE
    3.2. One Record Handle GHOST-MEM-IR-ONE-HANDLE Each record ingestion shall attempt to create one record_handle entry with block_id 0000 and position zero.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-PROJECT
      3.8. Record Handle Project Text GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-PROJECT The current record-handle embedded text shall include PROJECT derived from active_project_name.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-YAKE
      3.9. Record Handle Automatic Keywords GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-YAKE The current record-handle embedded text shall include YAKE_KEYWORDS derived from automatic keywords.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-QUESTION
      3.10. Record Handle Question Anchor GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-QUESTION The current record-handle embedded text shall include a QUESTION_ANCHOR derived from the first question block and bounded by question_anchor_tokens.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-QUESTION-ANCHOR-TRUNCATE
        4.8. Question Anchor Truncation GHOST-MEM-IR-QUESTION-ANCHOR-TRUNCATE Question-anchor truncation shall retain the first configured number of whitespace-delimited words.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-NO-TAG-TEXT
      3.11. Current Handle Text Exclusions GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-NO-TAG-TEXT The current record-handle embedded text shall not include tag or user_keywords, although those values may remain present in vector metadata.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-ROLES
      3.1. Vector Entry Roles GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-ROLES MemoryChunker shall produce vector entries with role record_handle, question, or answer.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-QUESTION-BLOCKS
    3.3. Question Entries GHOST-MEM-IR-QUESTION-BLOCKS MemoryChunker shall create ordered question entries from input_text, beginning with block_id 0001 and position one.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-ROLES
      3.1. Vector Entry Roles GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-ROLES MemoryChunker shall produce vector entries with role record_handle, question, or answer.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-ANSWER-BLOCKS
    3.4. Answer Entries GHOST-MEM-IR-ANSWER-BLOCKS MemoryChunker shall create ordered answer entries from output_text, beginning with block_id 0001 and position one.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-ROLES
      3.1. Vector Entry Roles GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-ROLES MemoryChunker shall produce vector entries with role record_handle, question, or answer.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-EMPTY-ENTRY-FILTER
    3.5. Empty Entry Filter GHOST-MEM-IR-EMPTY-ENTRY-FILTER A generated vector entry whose document text is empty after trimming shall be omitted from the returned entry list.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-ROLES
      3.1. Vector Entry Roles GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-ROLES MemoryChunker shall produce vector entries with role record_handle, question, or answer.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-ID-FORMAT
    3.6. Vector Entry Identifier GHOST-MEM-IR-ID-FORMAT The current vector identifier shall follow mem::<file_id>::<record_id>::<role>::<block_id>.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ID-TRACEABILITY
      3.7. Vector Parent Traceability GHOST-MEM-IR-ID-TRACEABILITY Every vector entry identifier and metadata shall link to file_id and record_id.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ID-FORMAT
      3.6. Vector Entry Identifier GHOST-MEM-IR-ID-FORMAT The current vector identifier shall follow mem::<file_id>::<record_id>::<role>::<block_id>.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-ROLES
        3.1. Vector Entry Roles GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-ROLES MemoryChunker shall produce vector entries with role record_handle, question, or answer.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.

    3.7. Vector Parent Traceability GHOST-MEM-IR-ID-TRACEABILITY Every vector entry identifier and metadata shall link to file_id and record_id.

      3.1. Vector Entry Roles GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-ROLES MemoryChunker shall produce vector entries with role record_handle, question, or answer.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.

          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-METADATA
    3.12. Vector Entry Metadata Contract GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-METADATA Each vector entry shall carry current file and record identifiers, paired filenames, parent_id, role, block identity and position, character offsets, approximate token count, tag, project, source, creation time, hashes, keyword text, embedded-file snapshot text, chunking configuration version, and ingestion hash.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-METADATA-SNAPSHOT
      3.13. Vector Metadata Snapshot GHOST-MEM-IR-METADATA-SNAPSHOT Vector metadata shall represent values at ingestion time and shall remain derivative rather than current metadata authority.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-LIVE-METADATA-OVERLAY
        3.14. Live Metadata during Retrieval GHOST-MEM-IR-LIVE-METADATA-OVERLAY When live MemoryRecords are available, retrieval scoring and candidate enrichment shall overlay live record metadata onto vector-derived metadata.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-METADATA
      3.12. Vector Entry Metadata Contract GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-METADATA Each vector entry shall carry current file and record identifiers, paired filenames, parent_id, role, block identity and position, character offsets, approximate token count, tag, project, source, creation time, hashes, keyword text, embedded-file snapshot text, chunking configuration version, and ingestion hash.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-ROLES
        3.1. Vector Entry Roles GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-ROLES MemoryChunker shall produce vector entries with role record_handle, question, or answer.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-METADATA-SNAPSHOT
    3.13. Vector Metadata Snapshot GHOST-MEM-IR-METADATA-SNAPSHOT Vector metadata shall represent values at ingestion time and shall remain derivative rather than current metadata authority.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-LIVE-METADATA-OVERLAY
      3.14. Live Metadata during Retrieval GHOST-MEM-IR-LIVE-METADATA-OVERLAY When live MemoryRecords are available, retrieval scoring and candidate enrichment shall overlay live record metadata onto vector-derived metadata.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-METADATA-SNAPSHOT
      3.13. Vector Metadata Snapshot GHOST-MEM-IR-METADATA-SNAPSHOT Vector metadata shall represent values at ingestion time and shall remain derivative rather than current metadata authority.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-METADATA
        3.12. Vector Entry Metadata Contract GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-METADATA Each vector entry shall carry current file and record identifiers, paired filenames, parent_id, role, block identity and position, character offsets, approximate token count, tag, project, source, creation time, hashes, keyword text, embedded-file snapshot text, chunking configuration version, and ingestion hash.

          3.1. Vector Entry Roles GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-ROLES MemoryChunker shall produce vector entries with role record_handle, question, or answer.

            1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.

              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-LIVE-METADATA-OVERLAY
    3.14. Live Metadata during Retrieval GHOST-MEM-IR-LIVE-METADATA-OVERLAY When live MemoryRecords are available, retrieval scoring and candidate enrichment shall overlay live record metadata onto vector-derived metadata.

    4. 4. Memory Chunking and Ingestion Hash
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-CHUNKER-CONFIG
    4.1. Current Chunker Configuration GHOST-MEM-IR-CHUNKER-CONFIG MemoryChunker shall maintain current defaults config_version memory_chunker_001, target_tokens 500, max_tokens 800, and question_anchor_tokens 220, with optional constructor overrides.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-TOKEN-APPROX
      4.2. Approximate Token Counting GHOST-MEM-IR-TOKEN-APPROX Current MemoryChunker token counts shall be the number of non-whitespace word matches and shall not be described as exact provider-token counts.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-PARAGRAPH-UNITS
      4.3. Paragraph Unit Selection GHOST-MEM-IR-PARAGRAPH-UNITS Paragraphs not exceeding max_tokens shall be used as semantic units.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-SENTENCE-UNITS
      4.4. Oversized Paragraph Sentence Units GHOST-MEM-IR-SENTENCE-UNITS An oversized paragraph shall be split into current sentence-like units based on punctuation or line endings.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-HARD-SPLIT
      4.5. Oversized Unit Hard Split GHOST-MEM-IR-HARD-SPLIT A semantic unit exceeding max_tokens shall be hard-split by non-whitespace word groups of at most max_tokens approximate tokens.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-BLOCK-TARGET
      4.6. Target Block Completion GHOST-MEM-IR-BLOCK-TARGET Current units shall accumulate until target_tokens is reached or the next unit would exceed max_tokens.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-BLOCK-OFFSETS
      4.7. Block Offsets GHOST-MEM-IR-BLOCK-OFFSETS Every question or answer block shall retain start_offset and end_offset relative to its source side.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-CHUNK-CONFIG-JSON-DIRECTION
      4.11. Chunker JSON Migration Direction GHOST-MEM-IR-CHUNK-CONFIG-JSON-DIRECTION Memory chunk sizes, anchor limit, and chunker version are operational tuning values and shall migrate to validated versioned JSON configuration before being claimed as centrally configured.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-CHUNKER-CONFIG
      4.1. Current Chunker Configuration GHOST-MEM-IR-CHUNKER-CONFIG MemoryChunker shall maintain current defaults config_version memory_chunker_001, target_tokens 500, max_tokens 800, and question_anchor_tokens 220, with optional constructor overrides.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-TOKEN-APPROX
    4.2. Approximate Token Counting GHOST-MEM-IR-TOKEN-APPROX Current MemoryChunker token counts shall be the number of non-whitespace word matches and shall not be described as exact provider-token counts.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-CHUNKER-CONFIG

        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-BLOCK-OFFSETS
    4.7. Block Offsets GHOST-MEM-IR-BLOCK-OFFSETS Every question or answer block shall retain start_offset and end_offset relative to its source side.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-QUESTION
      3.10. Record Handle Question Anchor GHOST-MEM-IR-HANDLE-QUESTION The current record-handle embedded text shall include a QUESTION_ANCHOR derived from the first question block and bounded by question_anchor_tokens.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ONE-HANDLE
        3.2. One Record Handle GHOST-MEM-IR-ONE-HANDLE Each record ingestion shall attempt to create one record_handle entry with block_id 0000 and position zero.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-ROLES
          3.1. Vector Entry Roles GHOST-MEM-IR-ENTRY-ROLES MemoryChunker shall produce vector entries with role record_handle, question, or answer.
          • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
            1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-QUESTION-ANCHOR-TRUNCATE
    4.8. Question Anchor Truncation GHOST-MEM-IR-QUESTION-ANCHOR-TRUNCATE Question-anchor truncation shall retain the first configured number of whitespace-delimited words.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-INGESTION-HASH
    4.9. Ingestion Hash GHOST-MEM-IR-INGESTION-HASH MemoryChunker shall compute SHA-256 over a deterministic JSON payload describing file_id, record_id, input/output hashes, tag, automatic/user keywords, active project, and chunking configuration version.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-INGESTION-HASH-NOT-TRUTH
      4.10. Ingestion Hash Status GHOST-MEM-IR-INGESTION-HASH-NOT-TRUTH The ingestion hash shall identify the derivative ingestion representation and shall not replace input_hash, output_hash, record_id, or file_id.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-INGESTION-HASH
      4.9. Ingestion Hash GHOST-MEM-IR-INGESTION-HASH MemoryChunker shall compute SHA-256 over a deterministic JSON payload describing file_id, record_id, input/output hashes, tag, automatic/user keywords, active project, and chunking configuration version.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-INGESTION-HASH-NOT-TRUTH
    4.10. Ingestion Hash Status GHOST-MEM-IR-INGESTION-HASH-NOT-TRUTH The ingestion hash shall identify the derivative ingestion representation and shall not replace input_hash, output_hash, record_id, or file_id.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-CHUNKER-CONFIG
      4.1. Current Chunker Configuration GHOST-MEM-IR-CHUNKER-CONFIG MemoryChunker shall maintain current defaults config_version memory_chunker_001, target_tokens 500, max_tokens 800, and question_anchor_tokens 220, with optional constructor overrides.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-CHUNK-CONFIG-JSON-DIRECTION
    4.11. Chunker JSON Migration Direction GHOST-MEM-IR-CHUNK-CONFIG-JSON-DIRECTION Memory chunk sizes, anchor limit, and chunker version are operational tuning values and shall migrate to validated versioned JSON configuration before being claimed as centrally configured.

    5. 5. Dedicated Memory Vector Store
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-PERSISTENT
    5.1. Persistent Chroma Store GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-PERSISTENT MemoryVectorStore shall create its persistence directory and open or create one named Chroma collection.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-NO-TRUTH
      5.2. Vector Store Non-Authority GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-NO-TRUTH MemoryVectorStore shall not own MemoryRecord truth, SQLite metadata authority, memory scoring, or MemoryContextPack construction.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-REPLACE-ID
      5.3. Record Replacement Identifier Validation GHOST-MEM-IR-REPLACE-ID replace_record_entries shall reject an empty record_id.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-REPLACE-DELETE-FIRST
      5.4. Current Record Replacement Order GHOST-MEM-IR-REPLACE-DELETE-FIRST Current record replacement shall count and delete all existing vectors for record_id before embedding and adding the new entry set.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-REPLACEMENT-ATOMICITY
        5.16. Current Replacement Transaction Boundary GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-REPLACEMENT-ATOMICITY Current delete-then-add replacement shall not be described as one atomic storage transaction; durable MemoryRecord truth shall provide failure isolation when vector replacement is interrupted.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-REPLACE-EMPTY
      5.5. Empty Replacement Entry Set GHOST-MEM-IR-REPLACE-EMPTY When replacement receives no entries, it shall leave the record with zero vectors and return a successful zero-write report after deleting prior vectors.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-DOCUMENTS
      5.6. Embedded Documents GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-DOCUMENTS The memory vector collection shall persist the vector entry document text together with embeddings and sanitized metadata.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-METADATA-SANITIZE
      5.7. Metadata Sanitization GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-METADATA-SANITIZE Vector metadata shall be converted to Chroma-compatible scalar values; non-scalar values shall be JSON-serialized and None shall become an empty string.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-EMBED
      5.8. Document Embedding GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-EMBED MemoryVectorStore shall obtain one embedding per vector-entry document through its configured embedder and normalize returned vectors to lists of floats.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-ADD
      5.9. Vector Add Contract GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-ADD After prior record vectors are deleted, current replacement shall add aligned IDs, documents, embeddings, and metadata to the collection.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-REPLACE-REPORT
      5.10. Replacement Report GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-REPLACE-REPORT Record replacement shall report success, record_id, deleted prior vector count, vectors written, resulting record vector count, collection name, and persistence directory.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-QUERY-EMPTY
      5.11. Empty Store Query GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-QUERY-EMPTY MemoryVectorStore.query shall return an empty hit list when query_text is empty after trimming.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-QUERY
      5.12. Store Query Contract GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-QUERY MemoryVectorStore.query shall embed query_text, query the collection with optional sanitized flat metadata filter, and return ordered flat hits containing id, document, metadata, distance, and one-based rank.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-DELETE-RECORD
      5.13. Delete Record Vectors GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-DELETE-RECORD MemoryVectorStore.delete_record shall delete vectors by record_id and report deleted and remaining counts.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-DELETE-FILE
      5.14. Delete History Vectors GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-DELETE-FILE MemoryVectorStore.delete_file shall delete vectors by file_id and report deleted and remaining file-level counts.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-COUNT-SAFE
      5.15. Vector Count Failure Behavior GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-COUNT-SAFE Current count_record and count_file helpers shall return zero when collection counting raises an exception.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-PERSISTENT
      5.1. Persistent Chroma Store GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-PERSISTENT MemoryVectorStore shall create its persistence directory and open or create one named Chroma collection.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.

    5.2. Vector Store Non-Authority GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-NO-TRUTH MemoryVectorStore shall not own MemoryRecord truth, SQLite metadata authority, memory scoring, or MemoryContextPack construction.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-PERSISTENT
      5.1. Persistent Chroma Store GHOST-MEM-IR-STORE-PERSISTENT MemoryVectorStore shall create its persistence directory and open or create one named Chroma collection.

    6. 6. Memory Ingestion Orchestration
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-DEPS
    6.1. Ingestion Dependencies GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-DEPS MemoryIngestionManager shall receive the active MemoryManager, MemoryChunker, and MemoryVectorStore.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-ID
    6.2. Ingest Record Identifier Validation GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-ID ingest_record shall return an unsuccessful result when record_id is empty.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-LOOKUP
    6.3. Live Record Lookup GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-LOOKUP ingest_record shall resolve record_id from the active MemoryManager.records list and shall report unsuccessful status when not found.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-BUILD
    6.4. Vector Entry Construction GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-BUILD For a found record, ingestion shall call MemoryChunker.build_vector_entries with current file_id and paired filenames.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-ROLE-COUNTS
    6.5. Ingestion Role Counts GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-ROLE-COUNTS Ingestion shall count returned entries by metadata role for diagnostics and result reporting.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-REPLACE
    6.6. Record Vector Replacement GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-REPLACE Ingestion shall replace all vectors for the record_id using the newly generated entry set.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-IDEMPOTENT-REPRESENTATION
      6.16. Record-Level Re-Ingestion Idempotency GHOST-MEM-IR-IDEMPOTENT-REPRESENTATION Re-ingesting a record shall replace its prior record_id vectors rather than intentionally append duplicate representations.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-IDEMPOTENT
        17.4. AC: Record Re-Ingestion GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-IDEMPOTENT Re-ingesting the same record_id shall replace prior vectors and shall not increase the number of duplicate IDs for that record.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-RESULT
    6.7. Ingestion Result Enrichment GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-RESULT The ingestion result shall include role counts, current file_id, and active .ragmem filename in addition to store replacement data.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-EXCEPTION
    6.8. Ingestion Exception Handling GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-EXCEPTION An exception during vector-entry construction or store replacement shall return an unsuccessful record-level result and shall not modify durable MemoryRecord files.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-ALL
    6.9. Loaded History Bulk Ingestion GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-ALL ingest_all shall iterate the current active MemoryManager record list in order, invoke ingest_record for each record, and report total, success, failure, and individual results.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-ALL-STATUS
      6.10. Bulk Ingestion Success Status GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-ALL-STATUS The bulk ingestion success field shall be true only when failure_count is zero.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-ALL
      6.9. Loaded History Bulk Ingestion GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-ALL ingest_all shall iterate the current active MemoryManager record list in order, invoke ingest_record for each record, and report total, success, failure, and individual results.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-ALL-STATUS
    6.10. Bulk Ingestion Success Status GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-ALL-STATUS The bulk ingestion success field shall be true only when failure_count is zero.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-SCHEDULE
    6.11. Asynchronous Record Ingestion GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-SCHEDULE ingest_record_async shall schedule one daemon thread for a valid active record_id.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-DUPLICATE
      6.12. Duplicate Async Suppression GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-DUPLICATE A process-local lock and active-record identifier set shall prevent concurrent duplicate scheduling for the same record_id within one MemoryIngestionManager instance.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-CLEANUP
      6.13. Async Active-Set Cleanup GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-CLEANUP The asynchronous worker shall remove record_id from the active set in a finally block.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-STREAMLIT-CONTEXT
      6.14. Optional Streamlit Thread Context GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-STREAMLIT-CONTEXT When available, current scheduling shall attach the active Streamlit script context to the ingestion thread; absence of that support shall not block thread start.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-NO-DURABLE-QUEUE
      6.15. No Durable Ingestion Queue Claim GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-NO-DURABLE-QUEUE The current daemon-thread scheduler shall not be described as a durable queue, retry service, or cross-process ingestion coordinator.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-SCHEDULE
      6.11. Asynchronous Record Ingestion GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-SCHEDULE ingest_record_async shall schedule one daemon thread for a valid active record_id.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-DUPLICATE
    6.12. Duplicate Async Suppression GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-DUPLICATE A process-local lock and active-record identifier set shall prevent concurrent duplicate scheduling for the same record_id within one MemoryIngestionManager instance.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-SCHEDULE
      6.11. Asynchronous Record Ingestion GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-SCHEDULE ingest_record_async shall schedule one daemon thread for a valid active record_id.

        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.

    6.13. Async Active-Set Cleanup GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-CLEANUP The asynchronous worker shall remove record_id from the active set in a finally block.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-SCHEDULE
      6.11. Asynchronous Record Ingestion GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-SCHEDULE ingest_record_async shall schedule one daemon thread for a valid active record_id.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-STREAMLIT-CONTEXT
    6.14. Optional Streamlit Thread Context GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-STREAMLIT-CONTEXT When available, current scheduling shall attach the active Streamlit script context to the ingestion thread; absence of that support shall not block thread start.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-SCHEDULE
      6.11. Asynchronous Record Ingestion GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-SCHEDULE ingest_record_async shall schedule one daemon thread for a valid active record_id.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-NO-DURABLE-QUEUE
    6.15. No Durable Ingestion Queue Claim GHOST-MEM-IR-ASYNC-NO-DURABLE-QUEUE The current daemon-thread scheduler shall not be described as a durable queue, retry service, or cross-process ingestion coordinator.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-REPLACE
      6.6. Record Vector Replacement GHOST-MEM-IR-INGEST-REPLACE Ingestion shall replace all vectors for the record_id using the newly generated entry set.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.

          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-IDEMPOTENT-REPRESENTATION
    6.16. Record-Level Re-Ingestion Idempotency GHOST-MEM-IR-IDEMPOTENT-REPRESENTATION Re-ingesting a record shall replace its prior record_id vectors rather than intentionally append duplicate representations.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-IDEMPOTENT
      17.4. AC: Record Re-Ingestion GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-IDEMPOTENT Re-ingesting the same record_id shall replace prior vectors and shall not increase the number of duplicate IDs for that record.

    7. 7. Memory Retrieval Input and Routing Preconditions
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVER-DEPS
    7.1. MemoryRetriever Dependencies GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVER-DEPS MemoryRetriever shall construct MemoryIndexLookup, MemoryScorer, MemoryCompressor, and MemoryMergeSynthesizer from its supplied dependencies and configuration.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-SHAPE
      7.2. Full or Section Configuration Input GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-SHAPE MemoryRetriever shall accept either the full runtime configuration containing memory_retrieval or the memory_retrieval section itself.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-ENABLED
      7.3. Memory Retrieval Enable Switch GHOST-MEM-IR-ENABLED When memory_retrieval.enabled is false, MemoryRetriever.run shall skip its processing and return the SuperPrompt unchanged.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-MEMORY-PERMISSION
      7.4. PreProcessing Memory Permission GHOST-MEM-IR-MEMORY-PERMISSION Memory retrieval and Memory Context synthesis shall honor the deterministic allow_memory_context and memory_context_policy prepared by PreProcessing.

      A disabled memory-context policy shall suppress memory context creation rather than allow unrelated memory to enter the final prompt.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-PERMISSION
        17.15. AC: Memory Context Permission GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-PERMISSION When PreProcessing sets allow_memory_context false, the final SuperPrompt shall not receive synthesized memory context for that request.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-MISSING-FILE-ID
      7.5. Missing Active file_id GHOST-MEM-IR-MISSING-FILE-ID When active file_id is empty, MemoryRetriever shall write an empty MemoryContextPack with reason no_active_memory_file.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PRIMARY
      7.6. Primary Memory Query GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PRIMARY MemoryRetriever shall use non-empty SuperPrompt.effective_retrieval_query_text as its primary query source.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PROJECTOR
        7.7. Projected Query Fallback GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PROJECTOR When the effective query is empty, MemoryRetriever shall attempt SuperPromptProjector.build_query_text.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-BODY-FALLBACK
        7.8. Body Query Fallback GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-BODY-FALLBACK If projector query construction fails, current fallback shall concatenate non-empty task, purpose, context, and text body fields in that order.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PROMPT-FALLBACK
        7.9. Prompt-Ready Final Fallback GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PROMPT-FALLBACK If no fallback body fields are available, current query construction may use non-empty prompt_ready.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-EMPTY-QUERY
      7.10. Empty Query Result GHOST-MEM-IR-EMPTY-QUERY When no query text can be built, MemoryRetriever shall write an empty pack with reason empty_query_text.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-KEY-INPUT
      7.11. Direct Recall Key Input GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-KEY-INPUT Current MemoryRetriever shall inspect SuperPrompt extras and body for direct_recall_key or memory_direct_recall_key and shall use the first non-empty trimmed value.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-KEY-NO-UI
        7.12. Current Direct Recall Query UI Boundary GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-KEY-NO-UI The current GUI does not require a separate Direct Recall query field; an absent key shall produce no Direct Recall lookup.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-LATEST-BRIEF
      7.13. Latest Eligible ActiveBrief Snapshot GHOST-MEM-IR-LATEST-BRIEF MemoryRetriever shall scan active records newest-to-oldest and select the latest non-Black record with a non-empty ActiveRetrievalBrief as the runtime brief snapshot.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVER-DEPS
      7.1. MemoryRetriever Dependencies GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVER-DEPS MemoryRetriever shall construct MemoryIndexLookup, MemoryScorer, MemoryCompressor, and MemoryMergeSynthesizer from its supplied dependencies and configuration.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-SHAPE
    7.2. Full or Section Configuration Input GHOST-MEM-IR-CONFIG-SHAPE MemoryRetriever shall accept either the full runtime configuration containing memory_retrieval or the memory_retrieval section itself.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVER-DEPS
      7.1. MemoryRetriever Dependencies GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVER-DEPS MemoryRetriever shall construct MemoryIndexLookup, MemoryScorer, MemoryCompressor, and MemoryMergeSynthesizer from its supplied dependencies and configuration.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.

    GHOST-MEM-IR-ENABLED
    7.3. Memory Retrieval Enable Switch GHOST-MEM-IR-ENABLED When memory_retrieval.enabled is false, MemoryRetriever.run shall skip its processing and return the SuperPrompt unchanged.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVER-DEPS
      7.1. MemoryRetriever Dependencies GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVER-DEPS MemoryRetriever shall construct MemoryIndexLookup, MemoryScorer, MemoryCompressor, and MemoryMergeSynthesizer from its supplied dependencies and configuration.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.

    GHOST-MEM-IR-MEMORY-PERMISSION
    7.4. PreProcessing Memory Permission GHOST-MEM-IR-MEMORY-PERMISSION Memory retrieval and Memory Context synthesis shall honor the deterministic allow_memory_context and memory_context_policy prepared by PreProcessing.

    A disabled memory-context policy shall suppress memory context creation rather than allow unrelated memory to enter the final prompt.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-PERMISSION
      17.15. AC: Memory Context Permission GHOST-MEM-IR-AC-PERMISSION When PreProcessing sets allow_memory_context false, the final SuperPrompt shall not receive synthesized memory context for that request.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVER-DEPS
      7.1. MemoryRetriever Dependencies GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVER-DEPS MemoryRetriever shall construct MemoryIndexLookup, MemoryScorer, MemoryCompressor, and MemoryMergeSynthesizer from its supplied dependencies and configuration.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-MISSING-FILE-ID
    7.5. Missing Active file_id GHOST-MEM-IR-MISSING-FILE-ID When active file_id is empty, MemoryRetriever shall write an empty MemoryContextPack with reason no_active_memory_file.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVER-DEPS
      7.1. MemoryRetriever Dependencies GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVER-DEPS MemoryRetriever shall construct MemoryIndexLookup, MemoryScorer, MemoryCompressor, and MemoryMergeSynthesizer from its supplied dependencies and configuration.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.

          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PRIMARY
    7.6. Primary Memory Query GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PRIMARY MemoryRetriever shall use non-empty SuperPrompt.effective_retrieval_query_text as its primary query source.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PROJECTOR
      7.7. Projected Query Fallback GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PROJECTOR When the effective query is empty, MemoryRetriever shall attempt SuperPromptProjector.build_query_text.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-BODY-FALLBACK
      7.8. Body Query Fallback GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-BODY-FALLBACK If projector query construction fails, current fallback shall concatenate non-empty task, purpose, context, and text body fields in that order.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PROMPT-FALLBACK
      7.9. Prompt-Ready Final Fallback GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PROMPT-FALLBACK If no fallback body fields are available, current query construction may use non-empty prompt_ready.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PRIMARY
      7.6. Primary Memory Query GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PRIMARY MemoryRetriever shall use non-empty SuperPrompt.effective_retrieval_query_text as its primary query source.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVER-DEPS
        7.1. MemoryRetriever Dependencies GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVER-DEPS MemoryRetriever shall construct MemoryIndexLookup, MemoryScorer, MemoryCompressor, and MemoryMergeSynthesizer from its supplied dependencies and configuration.
        • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PROJECTOR
    7.7. Projected Query Fallback GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PROJECTOR When the effective query is empty, MemoryRetriever shall attempt SuperPromptProjector.build_query_text.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PRIMARY
      7.6. Primary Memory Query GHOST-MEM-IR-QUERY-PRIMARY MemoryRetriever shall use non-empty SuperPrompt.effective_retrieval_query_text as its primary query source.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVER-DEPS
        7.1. MemoryRetriever Dependencies GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVER-DEPS MemoryRetriever shall construct MemoryIndexLookup, MemoryScorer, MemoryCompressor, and MemoryMergeSynthesizer from its supplied dependencies and configuration.

          1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.

        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.

    GHOST-MEM-IR-EMPTY-QUERY
    7.10. Empty Query Result GHOST-MEM-IR-EMPTY-QUERY When no query text can be built, MemoryRetriever shall write an empty pack with reason empty_query_text.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVER-DEPS
      7.1. MemoryRetriever Dependencies GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVER-DEPS MemoryRetriever shall construct MemoryIndexLookup, MemoryScorer, MemoryCompressor, and MemoryMergeSynthesizer from its supplied dependencies and configuration.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.

    GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-KEY-INPUT
    7.11. Direct Recall Key Input GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-KEY-INPUT Current MemoryRetriever shall inspect SuperPrompt extras and body for direct_recall_key or memory_direct_recall_key and shall use the first non-empty trimmed value.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-KEY-NO-UI
      7.12. Current Direct Recall Query UI Boundary GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-KEY-NO-UI The current GUI does not require a separate Direct Recall query field; an absent key shall produce no Direct Recall lookup.
    • GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-KEY-INPUT
      7.11. Direct Recall Key Input GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-KEY-INPUT Current MemoryRetriever shall inspect SuperPrompt extras and body for direct_recall_key or memory_direct_recall_key and shall use the first non-empty trimmed value.
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVER-DEPS
        7.1. MemoryRetriever Dependencies GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVER-DEPS MemoryRetriever shall construct MemoryIndexLookup, MemoryScorer, MemoryCompressor, and MemoryMergeSynthesizer from its supplied dependencies and configuration.

          1.1. Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT GHOST shall derive traceable memory vector entries from durable MemoryRecords, persist them in a dedicated memory vector store, retrieve current-history semantic candidates, perform deterministic metadata lookups, score parent records and chunks, build a MemoryContextPack, and hand selected evidence to runtime compression and synthesis.

    GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-KEY-NO-UI
    7.12. Current Direct Recall Query UI Boundary GHOST-MEM-IR-DIRECT-KEY-NO-UI The current GUI does not require a separate Direct Recall query field; an absent key shall produce no Direct Recall lookup.

      7.1. MemoryRetriever Dependencies GHOST-MEM-IR-RETRIEVER-DEPS MemoryRetriever shall construct MemoryIndexLookup, MemoryScorer, MemoryCompressor, and MemoryMergeSynthesizer from its supplied dependencies and configuration.
